title: Rajmachi Fort
tagline: Rajmachi Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Rajmachi Fort (Killa) is a historical fort in the hills of the Sahyadri mountains (Western Ghats).
overview.description: Rajmachi Fort (Killa) is a historical fort in the hills of the Sahyadri mountains (Western Ghats). It consists of two twin fortresses Shriwardhan and Manaranjan, with a wide machi (plateau) surrounding the two Balekillas. Udhewadi is a small village of about 60 households (as per 2011 census report) situated on the machi, at the southern foot of Manaranjan Balekilla of Rajmachi Fort.

topPlaces.name: Kondana Caves
topPlaces.description: The Kondhana Caves are located in the small village of Kondhane, 33 km (21 mi) north of Lonavala and 16 km (9.9 mi) northwest of Karla Caves. This cave group has 16 Buddhist caves.

topPlaces.name: Somavadi
topPlaces.description: Somavadi is a village in India, situated in the Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 317 ha (783 acres).

topPlaces.name: Kusavali
topPlaces.description: Kusavali is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 366 ha (904 acres).

topPlaces.name: Jambhavali
topPlaces.description: Jambhavali is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 972 ha (2,402 acres).

topPlaces.name: Badhalawadi
topPlaces.description: Badhalawadi is a village in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It encompasses an area of 814.66 ha (2,013 acres).

topPlaces.name: Dhak Bahiri
topPlaces.description: Dhak Bahiri is a cave in the Sahyadri hill range, situated near the village Jambhivli off village-Malavali in the Pune district of India. It was used by pilgrims and sages for the pilgrimage to the god Bahiri.

topPlaces.name: Shirvata Dam
topPlaces.description: Shirvata Dam, is a gravity dam on Indrayani river near Maval, Pune district in State of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Khopoli
topPlaces.description: Khopoli (Marathi pronunciation: [kʰopoˈliː], ISO: Khōpōlī) is an industrial city in the Khalapur taluka of Raigad district, in the Indian state of Maharashtra, at the base of the Sahyadri mountains. Patalganga River, which is the tailrace channel of the Tata Hydroelectric Power Station, flows through Khopoli.

Khopoli (Marathi pronunciation: [kʰopoˈliː], ISO: Khōpōlī) is an industrial city in the Khalapur taluka of Raigad district, in the Indian state of Maharashtra, at the base of the Sahyadri mountains. Patalganga River, which is the tailrace channel of the Tata Hydroelectric Power Station, flows through Khopoli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajmachi Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajmachi Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajmachi Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (53 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajmachi Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajmachi Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajmachi Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Rajmachi Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rajmachi Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kondana Caves, Somavadi, Kusavali, Jambhavali, Badhalawadi.
